front: What is the primary purpose of a Data Definition Language (DDL)?
back: DDL is used to define the database structure or schema. It includes commands like CREATE, ALTER, and DROP to define, modify, and remove database objects such as tables, indexes, and views.

front: What is the main function of a Data Manipulation Language (DML)?
back: DML is used for managing data within schema objects. Its commands (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) allow users to retrieve, insert, delete, and modify data stored in the database.

front: Describe the Three-Schema Architecture of a DBMS.
back: This architecture aims to separate the user applications from the physical database. It consists of three levels: 1) Internal Level (physical storage details), 2) Conceptual Level (logical structure of the entire database), and 3) External Level (user views). This separation provides data independence.

Data independence is the ability to change the schema at one level of a database system without having to change the schema at the next higher level. It is achieved through the Three-Schema Architecture and is categorized into Logical Data Independence (immunity of external schemas to logical changes) and Physical Data Independence (immunity of conceptual schema to physical changes).

front: What is the role of a Data Control Language (DCL)?
back: DCL is used to control access to data in the database. It includes commands like GRANT and REVOKE, which are used to provide or remove user permissions and rights, ensuring security and authorizing users to perform specific tasks.
